## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#introduction"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Introduction</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#purpose-and-scope"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Purpose and Scope</span>

<span data-as="p">The Model Context Protocol provides authorization
capabilities at the transport level, enabling MCP clients to make
requests to restricted MCP servers on behalf of resource owners. This
specification defines the authorization flow for HTTP-based
transports.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#protocol-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Protocol Requirements</span>

<span data-as="p">Authorization is **OPTIONAL** for MCP implementations.
When supported:</span>

- Implementations using an HTTP-based transport **SHOULD** conform to
  this specification.
- Implementations using an STDIO transport **SHOULD NOT** follow this
  specification, and instead retrieve credentials from the environment.
- Implementations using alternative transports **MUST** follow
  established security best practices for their protocol.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#standards-compliance"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Standards Compliance</span>

<span data-as="p">This authorization mechanism is based on established
specifications listed below, but implements a selected subset of their
features to ensure security and interoperability while maintaining
simplicity:</span>

- OAuth 2.1 IETF DRAFT
  (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13"
  class="link" target="_blank"
  rel="noreferrer">draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13</a>)
- OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata
  (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8414" class="link"
  target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC8414</a>)
- OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration Protocol
  (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591" class="link"
  target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC7591</a>)
- OAuth 2.0 Protected Resource Metadata
  (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
  target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728</a>)
- OAuth Client ID Metadata Documents (<a
  href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00"
  class="link" target="_blank"
  rel="noreferrer">draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00</a>)

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#roles"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Roles</span>

<span data-as="p">A protected *MCP server* acts as an <a
href="https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13.html#name-roles"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 resource
server</a>, capable of accepting and responding to protected resource
requests using access tokens.</span> <span data-as="p">An *MCP client*
acts as an <a
href="https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13.html#name-roles"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 client</a>,
making protected resource requests on behalf of a resource owner.</span>
<span data-as="p">The *authorization server* is responsible for
interacting with the user (if necessary) and issuing access tokens for
use at the MCP server. The implementation details of the authorization
server are beyond the scope of this specification. It may be hosted with
the resource server or a separate entity. The
<a href="authorization.html#authorization-server-discovery"
class="link">Authorization Server Discovery section</a> specifies how an
MCP server indicates the location of its corresponding authorization
server to a client.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#overview"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Overview</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">Authorization servers **MUST** implement OAuth 2.1
    with appropriate security measures for both confidential and public
    clients.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">Authorization servers and MCP clients **SHOULD**
    support OAuth Client ID Metadata Documents (<a
    href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00"
    class="link" target="_blank"
    rel="noreferrer">draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00</a>).</span>
3.  <span data-as="p">Authorization servers and MCP clients **MAY**
    support the OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration Protocol
    (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591" class="link"
    target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC7591</a>).</span>
4.  <span data-as="p">MCP servers **MUST** implement OAuth 2.0 Protected
    Resource Metadata
    (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
    target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728</a>). MCP clients **MUST**
    use OAuth 2.0 Protected Resource Metadata for authorization server
    discovery.</span>
5.  <span data-as="p">MCP authorization servers **MUST** provide at
    least one of the following discovery mechanisms:</span>
    - OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata
      (<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8414" class="link"
      target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC8414</a>)
    - <a href="https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html"
      class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OpenID Connect Discovery
      1.0</a>

    <span data-as="p">MCP clients **MUST** support both discovery
    mechanisms to obtain the information required to interact with the
    authorization server.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#authorization-server-discovery"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Server Discovery</span>

<span data-as="p">This section describes the mechanisms by which MCP
servers advertise their associated authorization servers to MCP clients,
as well as the discovery process through which MCP clients can determine
authorization server endpoints and supported capabilities.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#authorization-server-location"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Server Location</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP servers **MUST** implement the OAuth 2.0 Protected
Resource Metadata
(<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728</a>) specification to indicate
the locations of authorization servers. The Protected Resource Metadata
document returned by the MCP server **MUST** include the
`authorization_servers` field containing at least one authorization
server.</span> <span data-as="p">The specific use of
`authorization_servers` is beyond the scope of this specification;
implementers should consult OAuth 2.0 Protected Resource Metadata
(<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728</a>) for guidance on
implementation details.</span> <span data-as="p">Implementors should
note that Protected Resource Metadata documents can define multiple
authorization servers. The responsibility for selecting which
authorization server to use lies with the MCP client, following the
guidelines specified in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728#name-authorization-servers"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728 Section 7.6
“Authorization Servers”</a>.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="authorization.html#protected-resource-metadata-discovery-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Protected Resource Metadata Discovery
Requirements</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP servers **MUST** implement one of the following
discovery mechanisms to provide authorization server location
information to MCP clients:</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">**WWW-Authenticate Header**: Include the resource
    metadata URL in the `WWW-Authenticate` HTTP header under
    `resource_metadata` when returning `401 Unauthorized` responses, as
    described in <a
    href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728#name-www-authenticate-response"
    class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728 Section 5.1</a>.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">**Well-Known URI**: Serve metadata at a well-known
    URI as specified in
    <a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
    target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC9728</a>. This can be
    either:</span>
    - At the path of the server’s MCP endpoint:
      `https://example.com/public/mcp` could host metadata at
      `https://example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/public/mcp`
    - At the root:
      `https://example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`

<span data-as="p">MCP clients **MUST** support both discovery mechanisms
and use the resource metadata URL from the parsed `WWW-Authenticate`
headers when present; otherwise, they **MUST** fall back to constructing
and requesting the well-known URIs in the order listed above.</span>
<span data-as="p">MCP servers **SHOULD** include a `scope` parameter in
the `WWW-Authenticate` header as defined in
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6750#section-3"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 6750 Section 3</a> to
indicate the scopes required for accessing the resource. This provides
clients with immediate guidance on the appropriate scopes to request
during authorization, following the principle of least privilege and
preventing clients from requesting excessive permissions.</span>
<span data-as="p">The scopes included in the `WWW-Authenticate`
challenge **MAY** match `scopes_supported`, be a subset or superset of
it, or an alternative collection that is neither a strict subset nor
superset. Clients **MUST NOT** assume any particular set relationship
between the challenged scope set and `scopes_supported`. Clients
**MUST** treat the scopes provided in the challenge as authoritative for
satisfying the current request. Servers **SHOULD** strive for
consistency in how they construct scope sets but they are not required
to surface every dynamically issued scope through
`scopes_supported`.</span> <span data-as="p">Example 401 response with
scope guidance:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="3" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="https://mcp.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource",
                         scope="files:read"
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients **MUST** be able to parse
`WWW-Authenticate` headers and respond appropriately to
`HTTP 401 Unauthorized` responses from the MCP server.</span>
<span data-as="p">If the `scope` parameter is absent, clients **SHOULD**
apply the fallback behavior defined in the
<a href="authorization.html#scope-selection-strategy" class="link">Scope
Selection Strategy</a> section.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#authorization-server-metadata-discovery"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Server Metadata
Discovery</span>

<span data-as="p">To handle different issuer URL formats and ensure
interoperability with both OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata and
OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 specifications, MCP clients **MUST**
attempt multiple well-known endpoints when discovering authorization
server metadata.</span> <span data-as="p">The discovery approach is
based on
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8414#section-3.1"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC8414 Section 3.1
“Authorization Server Metadata Request”</a> for OAuth 2.0 Authorization
Server Metadata discovery and
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8414#section-5"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC8414 Section 5
“Compatibility Notes”</a> for OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0
interoperability.</span> <span data-as="p">For issuer URLs with path
components (e.g., `https://auth.example.com/tenant1`), clients **MUST**
try endpoints in the following priority order:</span>

1.  OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata with path insertion:
    `https://auth.example.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/tenant1`
2.  OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 with path insertion:
    `https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration/tenant1`
3.  OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 path appending:
    `https://auth.example.com/tenant1/.well-known/openid-configuration`

<span data-as="p">For issuer URLs without path components (e.g.,
`https://auth.example.com`), clients **MUST** try:</span>

1.  OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata:
    `https://auth.example.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`
2.  OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0:
    `https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration`

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="authorization.html#authorization-server-discovery-sequence-diagram"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Server Discovery Sequence
Diagram</span>

<span data-as="p">The following diagram outlines an example flow:</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#client-registration-approaches"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Client Registration Approaches</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP supports three client registration mechanisms.
Choose based on your scenario:</span>

- **Client ID Metadata Documents**: When client and server have no prior
  relationship (most common)
- **Pre-registration**: When client and server have an existing
  relationship
- **Dynamic Client Registration**: For backwards compatibility or
  specific requirements

<span data-as="p">Clients supporting all options **SHOULD** follow the
following priority order:</span>

1.  Use pre-registered client information for the server if the client
    has it available
2.  Use Client ID Metadata Documents if the Authorization Server
    indicates if the server supports it (via
    `client_id_metadata_document_supported` in OAuth Authorization
    Server Metadata)
3.  Use Dynamic Client Registration as a fallback if the Authorization
    Server supports it (via `registration_endpoint` in OAuth
    Authorization Server Metadata)
4.  Prompt the user to enter the client information if no other option
    is available

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#client-id-metadata-documents"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Client ID Metadata Documents</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients and authorization servers **SHOULD**
support OAuth Client ID Metadata Documents as specified in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth Client ID Metadata
Document</a>. This approach enables clients to use HTTPS URLs as client
identifiers, where the URL points to a JSON document containing client
metadata. This addresses the common MCP scenario where servers and
clients have no pre-existing relationship.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#implementation-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Implementation Requirements</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP implementations supporting Client ID Metadata
Documents **MUST** follow the requirements specified in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth Client ID Metadata
Document</a>. Key requirements include:</span> <span data-as="p">**For
MCP Clients:**</span>

- Clients **MUST** host their metadata document at an HTTPS URL
  following RFC requirements
- The `client_id` URL **MUST** use the “https” scheme and contain a path
  component, e.g. `https://example.com/client.json`
- The metadata document **MUST** include at least the following
  properties: `client_id`, `client_name`, `redirect_uris`
- Clients **MUST** ensure the `client_id` value in the metadata matches
  the document URL exactly
- Clients **MAY** use `private_key_jwt` for client authentication (e.g.,
  for requests to the token endpoint) with appropriate JWKS
  configuration as described in <a
  href="https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00.html#section-6.2"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Section 6.2 of Client ID
  Metadata Document</a>

<span data-as="p">**For Authorization Servers:**</span>

- **SHOULD** fetch metadata documents when encountering URL-formatted
  client_ids
- **MUST** validate that the fetched document’s `client_id` matches the
  URL exactly
- **SHOULD** cache metadata respecting HTTP cache headers
- **MUST** validate redirect URIs presented in an authorization request
  against those in the metadata document
- **MUST** validate the document structure is valid JSON and contains
  required fields
- **SHOULD** follow the security considerations in <a
  href="https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00.html#section-6"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Section 6 of Client ID
  Metadata Document</a>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#example-metadata-document"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Example Metadata Document</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="13" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "client_id": "https://app.example.com/oauth/client-metadata.json",
  "client_name": "Example MCP Client",
  "client_uri": "https://app.example.com",
  "logo_uri": "https://app.example.com/logo.png",
  "redirect_uris": [
    "http://127.0.0.1:3000/callback",
    "http://localhost:3000/callback"
  ],
  "grant_types": ["authorization_code"],
  "response_types": ["code"],
  "token_endpoint_auth_method": "none"
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#client-id-metadata-documents-flow"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Client ID Metadata Documents Flow</span>

<span data-as="p">The following diagram illustrates the complete flow
when using Client ID Metadata Documents:</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#discovery"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Discovery</span>

<span data-as="p">Authorization servers advertise that they support
clients using Client ID Metadata Documents by including the following
property in their OAuth Authorization Server metadata:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="3" language="json">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "client_id_metadata_document_supported": true
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients **SHOULD** check for this capability and
**MAY** fall back to Dynamic Client Registration or pre-registration if
unavailable.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#preregistration"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Preregistration</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients **SHOULD** support an option for static
client credentials such as those supplied by a preregistration flow.
This could be:</span>

1.  Hardcode a client ID (and, if applicable, client credentials)
    specifically for the MCP client to use when interacting with that
    authorization server, or
2.  Present a UI to users that allows them to enter these details, after
    registering an OAuth client themselves (e.g., through a
    configuration interface hosted by the server).

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#dynamic-client-registration"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Dynamic Client Registration</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients and authorization servers **MAY** support
the OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration Protocol
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC7591</a> to allow MCP clients to
obtain OAuth client IDs without user interaction. This option is
included for backwards compatibility with earlier versions of the MCP
authorization spec.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#scope-selection-strategy"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Scope Selection Strategy</span>

<span data-as="p">When implementing authorization flows, MCP clients
**SHOULD** follow the principle of least privilege by requesting only
the scopes necessary for their intended operations. During the initial
authorization handshake, MCP clients **SHOULD** follow this priority
order for scope selection:</span>

1.  **Use `scope` parameter** from the initial `WWW-Authenticate` header
    in the 401 response, if provided
2.  **If `scope` is not available**, use all scopes defined in
    `scopes_supported` from the Protected Resource Metadata document,
    omitting the `scope` parameter if `scopes_supported` is undefined.

<span data-as="p">This approach accommodates the general-purpose nature
of MCP clients, which typically lack domain-specific knowledge to make
informed decisions about individual scope selection. Requesting all
available scopes allows the authorization server and end-user to
determine appropriate permissions during the consent process.</span>
<span data-as="p">This approach minimizes user friction while following
the principle of least privilege. The `scopes_supported` field is
intended to represent the minimal set of scopes necessary for basic
functionality (see <a
href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/security_best_practices#scope-minimization"
class="link">Scope Minimization</a>), with additional scopes requested
incrementally through the step-up authorization flow steps described in
the
<a href="authorization.html#scope-challenge-handling" class="link">Scope
Challenge Handling</a> section.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#authorization-flow-steps"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Flow Steps</span>

<span data-as="p">The complete Authorization flow proceeds as
follows:</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#resource-parameter-implementation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Resource Parameter Implementation</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients **MUST** implement Resource Indicators for
OAuth 2.0 as defined in
<a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8707.html" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 8707</a> to explicitly specify the
target resource for which the token is being requested. The `resource`
parameter:</span>

1.  **MUST** be included in both authorization requests and token
    requests.
2.  **MUST** identify the MCP server that the client intends to use the
    token with.
3.  **MUST** use the canonical URI of the MCP server as defined in <a
    href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8707.html#name-access-token-request"
    class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 8707 Section 2</a>.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#canonical-server-uri"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Canonical Server URI</span>

<span data-as="p">For the purposes of this specification, the canonical
URI of an MCP server is defined as the resource identifier as specified
in <a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8707.html#section-2"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 8707 Section 2</a> and
aligns with the `resource` parameter in
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 9728</a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">MCP clients **SHOULD** provide the most specific URI
that they can for the MCP server they intend to access, following the
guidance in
<a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8707" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 8707</a>. While the canonical form
uses lowercase scheme and host components, implementations **SHOULD**
accept uppercase scheme and host components for robustness and
interoperability.</span> <span data-as="p">Examples of valid canonical
URIs:</span>

- `https://mcp.example.com/mcp`
- `https://mcp.example.com`
- `https://mcp.example.com:8443`
- `https://mcp.example.com/server/mcp` (when path component is necessary
  to identify individual MCP server)

<span data-as="p">Examples of invalid canonical URIs:</span>

- `mcp.example.com` (missing scheme)
- `https://mcp.example.com#fragment` (contains fragment)

> <span data-as="p">**Note:** While both `https://mcp.example.com/`
> (with trailing slash) and `https://mcp.example.com` (without trailing
> slash) are technically valid absolute URIs according to
> <a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986" class="link"
> target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 3986</a>, implementations
> **SHOULD** consistently use the form without the trailing slash for
> better interoperability unless the trailing slash is semantically
> significant for the specific resource.</span>

<span data-as="p">For example, if accessing an MCP server at
`https://mcp.example.com`, the authorization request would
include:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="2" language="text">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
&resource=https%3A%2F%2Fmcp.example.com
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">MCP clients **MUST** send this parameter regardless of
whether authorization servers support it.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#access-token-usage"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Access Token Usage</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#token-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Token Requirements</span>

<span data-as="p">Access token handling when making requests to MCP
servers **MUST** conform to the requirements defined in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-5"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 5
“Resource Requests”</a>. Specifically:</span>

1.  MCP client **MUST** use the Authorization request header field
    defined in <a
    href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-5.1.1"
    class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section
    5.1.1</a>:

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="2" language="text">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
Authorization: Bearer <access-token>
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">Note that authorization **MUST** be included in every
HTTP request from client to server, even if they are part of the same
logical session.</span>

2.  Access tokens **MUST NOT** be included in the URI query string

<span data-as="p">Example request:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="3" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
GET /mcp HTTP/1.1
Host: mcp.example.com
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#token-handling"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Token Handling</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP servers, acting in their role as an OAuth 2.1
resource server, **MUST** validate access tokens as described in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-5.2"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 5.2</a>.
MCP servers **MUST** validate that access tokens were issued
specifically for them as the intended audience, according to
<a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8707.html#section-2"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 8707 Section 2</a>. If
validation fails, servers **MUST** respond according to <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-5.3"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 5.3</a>
error handling requirements. Invalid or expired tokens **MUST** receive
a HTTP 401 response.</span> <span data-as="p">MCP clients **MUST NOT**
send tokens to the MCP server other than ones issued by the MCP server’s
authorization server.</span> <span data-as="p">MCP servers **MUST** only
accept tokens that are valid for use with their own resources.</span>
<span data-as="p">MCP servers **MUST NOT** accept or transit any other
tokens.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#error-handling"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Error Handling</span>

<span data-as="p">Servers **MUST** return appropriate HTTP status codes
for authorization errors:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Status Code | Description  | Usage                                      |
|-------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------|
| 401         | Unauthorized | Authorization required or token invalid    |
| 403         | Forbidden    | Invalid scopes or insufficient permissions |
| 400         | Bad Request  | Malformed authorization request            |

</div>

</div>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#scope-challenge-handling"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Scope Challenge Handling</span>

<span data-as="p">This section covers handling insufficient scope errors
during runtime operations when a client already has a token but needs
additional permissions. This follows the error handling patterns defined
in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-5"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 5</a>
and leverages the metadata fields from
<a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 9728 (OAuth 2.0 Protected Resource
Metadata)</a>.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#runtime-insufficient-scope-errors"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Runtime Insufficient Scope Errors</span>

<span data-as="p">When a client makes a request with an access token
with insufficient scope during runtime operations, the server **SHOULD**
respond with:</span>

- `HTTP 403 Forbidden` status code (per
  <a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6750#section-3.1"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 6750 Section 3.1</a>)
- `WWW-Authenticate` header with the `Bearer` scheme and additional
  parameters:
  - `error="insufficient_scope"` - indicating the specific type of
    authorization failure
  - `scope="required_scope1 required_scope2"` - specifying the minimum
    scopes needed for the operation
  - `resource_metadata` - the URI of the Protected Resource Metadata
    document (for consistency with 401 responses)
  - `error_description` (optional) - human-readable description of the
    error

<span data-as="p">**Server Scope Management**: When responding with
insufficient scope errors, servers **SHOULD** include the scopes needed
to satisfy the current request in the `scope` parameter.</span>
<span data-as="p">Servers have flexibility in determining which scopes
to include:</span>

- **Minimum approach**: Include the newly-required scopes for the
  specific operation. Include any existing granted scopes as well, if
  they are required, to prevent clients from losing previously granted
  permissions.
- **Recommended approach**: Include both existing relevant scopes and
  newly required scopes to prevent clients from losing previously
  granted permissions
- **Extended approach**: Include existing scopes, newly required scopes,
  and related scopes that commonly work together

<span data-as="p">The choice depends on the server’s assessment of user
experience impact and authorization friction.</span>
<span data-as="p">Servers **SHOULD** be consistent in their scope
inclusion strategy to provide predictable behavior for clients.</span>
<span data-as="p">Servers **SHOULD** consider the user experience impact
when determining which scopes to include in the response, as
misconfigured scopes may require frequent user interaction.</span>
<span data-as="p">Example insufficient scope response:</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="5" language="http">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
WWW-Authenticate: Bearer error="insufficient_scope",
                         scope="files:read files:write user:profile",
                         resource_metadata="https://mcp.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource",
                         error_description="Additional file write permission required"
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#step-up-authorization-flow"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Step-Up Authorization Flow</span>

<span data-as="p">Clients will receive scope-related errors during
initial authorization or at runtime (`insufficient_scope`). Clients
**SHOULD** respond to these errors by requesting a new access token with
an increased set of scopes via a step-up authorization flow or handle
the errors in other, appropriate ways. Clients acting on behalf of a
user **SHOULD** attempt the step-up authorization flow. Clients acting
on their own behalf (`client_credentials` clients) **MAY** attempt the
step-up authorization flow or abort the request immediately.</span>
<span data-as="p">The flow is as follows:</span>

1.  **Parse error information** from the authorization server response
    or `WWW-Authenticate` header
2.  **Determine required scopes** as outlined in
    <a href="authorization.html#scope-selection-strategy" class="link">Scope
    Selection Strategy</a>.
3.  **Initiate (re-)authorization** with the determined scope set
4.  **Retry the original request** with the new authorization no more
    than a few times and treat this as a permanent authorization failure

<span data-as="p">Clients **SHOULD** implement retry limits and
**SHOULD** track scope upgrade attempts to avoid repeated failures for
the same resource and operation combination.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#security-considerations"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Security Considerations</span>

<span data-as="p">Implementations **MUST** follow OAuth 2.1 security
best practices as laid out in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#name-security-considerations"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 7.
“Security Considerations”</a>.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#token-audience-binding-and-validation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Token Audience Binding and
Validation</span>

<span data-as="p"><a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8707.html" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">RFC 8707</a> Resource Indicators
provide critical security benefits by binding tokens to their intended
audiences **when the Authorization Server supports the capability**. To
enable current and future adoption:</span>

- MCP clients **MUST** include the `resource` parameter in authorization
  and token requests as specified in the
  <a href="authorization.html#resource-parameter-implementation"
  class="link">Resource Parameter Implementation</a> section
- MCP servers **MUST** validate that tokens presented to them were
  specifically issued for their use

<span data-as="p">The <a
href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/security_best_practices#token-passthrough"
class="link">Security Best Practices document</a> outlines why token
audience validation is crucial and why token passthrough is explicitly
forbidden.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#token-theft"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Token Theft</span>

<span data-as="p">Attackers who obtain tokens stored by the client, or
tokens cached or logged on the server can access protected resources
with requests that appear legitimate to resource servers.</span>
<span data-as="p">Clients and servers **MUST** implement secure token
storage and follow OAuth best practices, as outlined in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-7.1"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1, Section 7.1</a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">Authorization servers **SHOULD** issue short-lived
access tokens to reduce the impact of leaked tokens. For public clients,
authorization servers **MUST** rotate refresh tokens as described in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-4.3.1"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 4.3.1
“Token Endpoint Extension”</a>.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#communication-security"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Communication Security</span>

<span data-as="p">Implementations **MUST** follow <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-1.5"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 1.5
“Communication Security”</a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">Specifically:</span>

1.  All authorization server endpoints **MUST** be served over HTTPS.
2.  All redirect URIs **MUST** be either `localhost` or use HTTPS.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#authorization-code-protection"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Code Protection</span>

<span data-as="p">An attacker who has gained access to an authorization
code contained in an authorization response can try to redeem the
authorization code for an access token or otherwise make use of the
authorization code. (Further described in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-7.5"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section 7.5</a>)</span>
<span data-as="p">To mitigate this, MCP clients **MUST** implement PKCE
according to <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-7.5.2"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section
7.5.2</a> and **MUST** verify PKCE support before proceeding with
authorization. PKCE helps prevent authorization code interception and
injection attacks by requiring clients to create a secret
verifier-challenge pair, ensuring that only the original requestor can
exchange an authorization code for tokens.</span> <span data-as="p">MCP
clients **MUST** use the `S256` code challenge method when technically
capable, as required by <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-4.1.1"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section
4.1.1</a>.</span> <span data-as="p">Since OAuth 2.1 and PKCE
specifications do not define a mechanism for clients to discover PKCE
support, MCP clients **MUST** rely on authorization server metadata to
verify this capability:</span>

- <span data-as="p">**OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server Metadata**: If
  `code_challenge_methods_supported` is absent, the authorization server
  does not support PKCE and MCP clients **MUST** refuse to
  proceed.</span>
- <span data-as="p">**OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0**: While the <a
  href="https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#ProviderMetadata"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OpenID Provider
  Metadata</a> does not define `code_challenge_methods_supported`, this
  field is commonly included by OpenID providers. MCP clients **MUST**
  verify the presence of `code_challenge_methods_supported` in the
  provider metadata response. If the field is absent, MCP clients
  **MUST** refuse to proceed.</span>

<span data-as="p">Authorization servers providing OpenID Connect
Discovery 1.0 **MUST** include `code_challenge_methods_supported` in
their metadata to ensure MCP compatibility.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#open-redirection"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Open Redirection</span>

<span data-as="p">An attacker may craft malicious redirect URIs to
direct users to phishing sites.</span> <span data-as="p">MCP clients
**MUST** have redirect URIs registered with the authorization
server.</span> <span data-as="p">Authorization servers **MUST** validate
exact redirect URIs against pre-registered values to prevent redirection
attacks.</span> <span data-as="p">MCP clients **SHOULD** use and verify
state parameters in the authorization code flow and discard any results
that do not include or have a mismatch with the original state.</span>
<span data-as="p">Authorization servers **MUST** take precautions to
prevent redirecting user agents to untrusted URI’s, following
suggestions laid out in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-v2-1-13#section-7.12.2"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth 2.1 Section
7.12.2</a></span> <span data-as="p">Authorization servers **SHOULD**
only automatically redirect the user agent if it trusts the redirection
URI. If the URI is not trusted, the authorization server MAY inform the
user and rely on the user to make the correct decision.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#client-id-metadata-document-security"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Client ID Metadata Document Security</span>

<span data-as="p">When implementing Client ID Metadata Documents,
authorization servers **MUST** consider the security implications
detailed in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00#name-security-considerations"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth Client ID Metadata
Document, Section 6</a>. Key considerations include:</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#authorization-server-abuse-protection"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Authorization Server Abuse
Protection</span>

<span data-as="p">The authorization server takes a URL as input from an
unknown client and fetches that URL. A malicious client could use this
to trigger the authorization server to make requests to arbitrary URLs,
such as requests to private administration endpoints the authorization
server has access to.</span> <span data-as="p">Authorization servers
fetching metadata documents **SHOULD** consider
<a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/Security/Attacks/SSRF"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">Server-Side Request
Forgery (SSRF)</a> risks, as described in <a
href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-client-id-metadata-document-00#name-server-side-request-forgery"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">OAuth Client ID Metadata
Document: Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Attacks</a>.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#localhost-redirect-uri-risks"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Localhost Redirect URI Risks</span>

<span data-as="p">Client ID Metadata Documents cannot prevent
`localhost` URL impersonation by themselves. An attacker can claim to be
any client by:</span>

1.  Providing the legitimate client’s metadata URL as their `client_id`
2.  Binding to the any `localhost` port, and providing that address as
    the redirect_uri
3.  Receiving the authorization code via the redirect when the user
    approves

<span data-as="p">The server will see the legitimate client’s metadata
document and the user will see the legitimate client’s name, making
attack detection difficult.</span> <span data-as="p">Authorization
servers:</span>

- **SHOULD** display additional warnings for `localhost`-only redirect
  URIs
- **MAY** require additional attestation mechanisms for enhanced
  security
- **MUST** clearly display the redirect URI hostname during
  authorization

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#trust-policies"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Trust Policies</span>

<span data-as="p">Authorization servers **MAY** implement domain-based
trust policies:</span>

- Allowlists for trusted domains (for protected servers)
- Accept any HTTPS `client_id` (for open servers)
- Reputation checks for unknown domains
- Restrictions based on domain age or certificate validation
- Display the CIMD and other associated client hostnames prominently to
  prevent phishing

<span data-as="p">Servers maintain full control over their access
policies.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="authorization.html#confused-deputy-problem"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Confused Deputy Problem</span>

<span data-as="p">Attackers can exploit MCP servers acting as
intermediaries to third-party APIs, leading to <a
href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/security_best_practices#confused-deputy-problem"
class="link">confused deputy vulnerabilities</a>. By using stolen
authorization codes, they can obtain access tokens without user
